purpose: Compact proof hierarchy map for /work, /work/[slug], and homepage proof selection. Use this to avoid flattening parent systems, over-promoting weak supporting work, or re-reading long proof docs.

flagshipWork.proofDirection: long-term leadership + healthcare growth + website trust

graston-technique.displayRule: Parent first. Child work belongs beneath or beside the parent story, never in the same visual tier by default.

pike-medical-consultants.displayRule: Use sub-brands as system proof, not as equal replacements for the parent healthcare story.

hoosier-boy-barbershop.displayRule: Use command-center proof to deepen the operational story, not to overshadow the flagship booking/local case study.

flagship-longform.notes: These deserve the fullest story structure: business context, intervention, proof media, results, and routing into services.

supporting-standard.notes: Keep these concise and strong. Do not over-architect them to feel like enterprise case-study theater.

system-compact.notes: These should explain the role of the build inside a larger system, what it solved, and what changed because it existed.

system-expanded.notes: Still tied to parent context, but strong enough to justify a richer story and clearer media handling.

homepageShowcaseBias.notes: Use a tighter, more commercially varied proof set on the homepage. Bias toward outcomes and range, not just visual contrast.

global: Prefer real product, website, dashboard, or workflow screens over logos whenever available.
Use logos only as support media, not as the hero proof surface.
Use parent-system media to reinforce hierarchy — child builds should not visually outrank their parent engagement by accident.

heroBias.barbershop-command-center: operational UI or booking OS screen

antiPatterns: Letting every child build compete with the parent system visually.
Using weaker but more visual work as the homepage proof just because it is eye-catching.
Forcing every work detail page into the same long-form template.
Using brand-only projects to carry system or conversion claims they do not actually prove.
